what_it_does: Acceptance test for the S2 event-address-stamp keystone. It exercises real Suite methods to enumerate emitted event kinds and asserts each event at an addressable locus carries an `address` field (run:// for graph/node events, ui://chrome/<region> for chrome/inbox/review events). It also AST-scans runtime/suite.py to verify every self._emit / self._emit_durable call site either passes `address=` or is in the documented locus-less exclusion set.
observations: The top docstring frames the test as targeting the partial-stamp failure mode, not a single-action test.
It enumerates emit kinds by calling real Suite methods against a temporary FsStore.
Two address schemes are distinguished: run://<graph>[/<node>] and ui://chrome/<region>.
Locus-less system-health warnings are explicitly excluded with summary-string markers and an excluded-kinds set.
apply_node is tested inside an isolated throwaway git repo to avoid worktree pollution.
An AST scan of runtime/suite.py checks that every _emit/_emit_durable call site carries `address=` or matches an exclusion marker.
Reader non-breakage checks verify decision_view and events_since still work after the additive `address` field.
Helpers check, last_of_kind, valid_address, and is_excluded drive the assertions.
standouts: The AST completeness scan turns a by-use acceptance test into a static proof that no relevant emitter call site is unstamped.
The explicit exclusion set is validated by asserting every marker matches a real unstamped call site, preventing silent allow-list drift.
The isolated git repo technique lets apply_node run for real without touching the project worktree.
conventions: Follows additive event-store semantics: events are open dicts and readers use .get.
Uses tempfile/shutil for store and repo isolation.
Uses a global PASS counter and a custom check helper instead of a standard test framework.
Breaks typical unit-test boundaries by statically parsing another module's source AST.
concerns: Exclusion matching depends on exact summary literals in runtime/suite.py, so wording changes will break the test.
Some address checks use broad startswith('ui://') rather than exact expected addresses.
The AST scan opens runtime/suite.py via a path derived from ROOT, making it sensitive to directory layout changes.
Long interleaved comments reduce readability of the test body.
Global mutable PASS counter is non-standard and could be fragile under parallel execution.
notes: An acceptance test that proves every addressable emit site stamps an `address` and documents the locus-less exceptions, backed by an AST scan of the emitter.
questions: What exact Suite methods and event kinds are defined in runtime/suite.py?
How stable are the summary literals used as exclusion markers?
What is the full registry resolution semantics for run:// and ui:// references?
What does the §21.7 re-key cluster require beyond this test?
Are there emitters outside runtime/suite.py that also need address-stamping verification?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose — enumerate emit kinds, assert each carries address, and AST-scan to prevent partial stamps — matches what the file does.
apparent_themes: Explicit boundaries for locus-less system events
intention_signals: It is trying to enforce that every human-relevant action in the event ledger carries a locative address so downstream layers can filter by locus, while honestly leaving system-health warnings unaddressed.
novelty: Combining runtime acceptance assertions with a static AST scan of the same emitter to guarantee no future call site is missed, framed as an adversarial defense against partial stamping.
contribution: A test harness and completeness proof for address-stamping that enumerates real emit kinds, documents locus-less exclusions, and provides a reusable pattern for verifying additive event fields across call sites.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test for S2 event-address stamping that exercises real Suite emit paths and AST-scans runtime/suite.py to ensure every _emit/_emit_durable call site either carries an address or is a documented locus-less health warning.
intention_for_embedding: To kill the partial-stamp failure mode by proving every addressable event kind carries a run:// or ui:// address and by statically verifying no relevant emitter call site is missed.

symbols.does: Assertion helper that increments PASS and prints ok, or raises a labeled failure.
symbols.description: The check function takes a label and a condition, asserts the condition with a FAIL label on failure, increments the global PASS counter on success, and prints an ok message.

symbols.does: Returns the most recent event of a given kind from the store tail.
symbols.description: last_of_kind filters store.events_since(-1) by the provided kind and returns the last matching event, using .get for additive-safe access so the new address field does not break it.

symbols.does: Checks whether a value is a non-empty run:// or ui:// string.
symbols.description: valid_address returns True when the input is a string starting with run:// or ui:// and longer than the run:// prefix, used to validate stamped event addresses.

symbols.does: Returns True if a summary matches any locus-less health-warning marker.
symbols.description: is_excluded checks whether the provided summary string contains any substring from EXCLUDED_SUMMARY_MARKERS, allowing the AST scan to treat corresponding unstamped _emit calls as documented exclusions.
